mailing_address: Lincoln Financial Group | P.O. Box 26068 | Greensboro, NC 27420
overnight_address: Lincoln Financial Group | 100 North Greene Street | Greensboro, NC 27401
overview: Overview Lincoln Financial Group is a leading provider of life insurance, annuities, and retirement plan services in the United States. When a policyholder or account holder passes away, the beneficiary or estate representative must notify Lincoln Financial to initiate the claim process. This guide covers Lincoln Financial's death claim process for individual life insurance, group life insurance (through an employer), annuities, and retirement accounts. Because Lincoln Financial operates multiple subsidiaries (such as The Lincoln National Life Insurance Company and Lincoln Life & Annuity Company of New York ), the specific forms and mailing addresses may vary slightly by policy type. Lincoln Financial offers an online claims portal which is often the fastest way to start the process. This guide provides informational guidance only. It is not legal advice, and SwiftProbate is not a law firm. Laws and institutional procedures change — verify current requirements directly with Lincoln Financial and consider consulting a qualified attorney.
notification_process: Notification Process How to Notify Lincoln Financial of a Death Step 1: Gather Initial Information Before contacting Lincoln Financial, have the following ready: Decedent's full legal name and date of birth Date of death Policy or account numbers (look for recent statements or premium bills) Social Security number of the decedent Your relationship to the decedent Beneficiary information (if you are the beneficiary) Step 2: Initiate the Claim You can start the process online or by phone. Option A: Online (Recommended) Visit the Lincoln Financial Claims Portal . You can report a death and start a life insurance or annuity claim directly through the site. Option B: Call the Claims Department Contact the specific department based on the product type: Individual Life Insurance & Annuities: (800) 487-1485 (Select Option 4) Group Life Insurance (Employer Plans): (800) 423-2765 Retirement Plans: (800) 348-4608 Hours: Mon–Fri, 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM ET When you reach an agent, they will verify the policy details and send you a claim packet (Claimant's Statement) via mail or email. Step 3: Submit Required Documents Complete the claim forms provided by the representative. You will typically need to mail these along with the death certificate. For Individual Life & Annuities: Lincoln Financial Group P.O. Box 26068 Greensboro, NC 27420 For Group Life Insurance: The Lincoln National Life Insurance Company P.O. Box 2616 Omaha, NE 68103 (Check the specific address listed on your claim packet, as it may differ based on the subsidiary holding the policy.) Step 4: Wait for Processing After Lincoln Financial receives your documents, expect: Document review: 5–10 business days Benefit processing: 10–15 business days after all requirements are met Payment issuance: Checks are typically mailed within 5 days of approval Step 5: Receive Funds Funds are typically distributed via: Lump sum check Lincoln SecuredPlus Account (a retained asset account for larger amounts) Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) (if selected on the claim form)
required_documents: Required Documents Documents Lincoln Financial Requires The exact documents depend on the policy type and the amount of the benefit. Always Required Document Details Certified death certificate One certified copy (Lincoln may return it upon request, but don't count on it) Claimant's Statement Form provided by Lincoln (e.g., Form CL06541 for life insurance) Valid Photo ID Copy of driver's license or passport for the beneficiary If the Estate is the Beneficiary Document Details Letters Testamentary Court-certified document appointing the Executor Letters of Administration Court-certified document appointing the Administrator (if no will) EIN (Tax ID) For the estate (required for interest reporting) If the Beneficiary is a Trust Document Details Trust Certification Verifying the trust's existence and the trustee's authority Trustee's ID Copy of the trustee's government-issued ID For Annuities Document Details Annuity Claim Form Specific to the contract type (Variable vs. Fixed) Spousal Continuation Form If the surviving spouse wishes to continue the contract
account_types: Account Types How Lincoln Financial Handles Different Account Types Individual Life Insurance Beneficiaries must file a claim to receive the death benefit. If the policy is within the contestability period (usually the first 2 years), the review process will take longer as Lincoln verifies medical history. Timeline: 10–15 business days (uncontested) Group Life Insurance (Employer-Sponsored) Claims are often initiated through the employer's HR department, but beneficiaries can also contact Lincoln directly. The employer may need to provide proof of active employment/enrollment. Timeline: 10–20 business days Annuities Surviving owners or beneficiaries have several options: Lump sum payout Spousal continuation (spouse takes over the contract) 5-year deferral or Life expectancy payments Timeline: 15–20 business days Retirement Plans (401k, 403b) Distribution depends on the plan rules and the beneficiary's relationship to the deceased. Spouses have more rollover options than non-spouse beneficiaries. Timeline: 15–30 business days Lincoln SecuredPlus Account For life insurance payouts over a certain threshold (often $5,000+), Lincoln may open an interest-bearing draft account in the beneficiary's name rather than sending a single check. You can write checks against this account to access funds.
state_considerations: State Considerations How Your State Affects the Lincoln Financial Process Lincoln Financial operates nationally, but state laws impact claims: Interest on Death Benefits: Many states (like IL, NY, PA) require insurers to pay interest on the death benefit from the date of death until payment. Contestability: State laws define the contestability period (usually 2 years) during which Lincoln can investigate the cause of death or application accuracy. Slayer Statutes: All states prevent a beneficiary from collecting if they are responsible for the insured's death. Community Property: In states like CA, TX, and AZ, a spouse may have a claim to a portion of the policy proceeds even if they are not the named beneficiary, if premiums were paid with community funds. Key State Variations State Interest Requirement Contestability Notable Rules New York Yes 2 Years Strict beneficiary protection rules California Yes 2 Years Community property interest applies Florida Yes 2 Years Creditor protection for life insurance proceeds For detailed probate requirements, see our state and county probate guides .
timelines: Timelines How Long Lincoln Financial Takes to Release Funds Account Type Estimated Timeline Key Factor Life Insurance (Simple) 10–15 business days After all forms & death cert received Life Insurance (Contestable) 2–6 months Medical record review required Group Life 10–20 business days Employer verification often needed Annuities 15–20 business days Complexity of distribution options Retirement Accounts 15–30 business days Plan administrator approval Estate as Beneficiary 4–8 weeks Depends on probate paperwork Note: Timelines start only after Lincoln Financial receives the completed claim packet and certified death certificate.
tips_and_pitfalls: Tips & Pitfalls Common Pitfalls and Tips Check for Multiple Policies The decedent may have had an individual policy and a group policy through work. Ask the agent to search for all policies linked to the decedent's Social Security number. Do Not Close the Account Too Early For annuities and retirement accounts, do not attempt to close the account or move funds before speaking with the estate department. This can trigger unintended taxable events. The "Retained Asset Account" Lincoln often pays large claims via a SecuredPlus Account (a checkbook linked to an account) rather than a single check. Tip: You can write one check for the full balance to move it to your own bank immediately if you prefer. Contestability Period If the policy was purchased less than 2 years ago, Lincoln will investigate the medical history. Tip: Be prepared to sign HIPAA release forms to allow them to access medical records. Interest Payments Check if your state requires interest on the death benefit. Lincoln should include this automatically, but it's worth verifying on the final statement.
faq: Frequently Asked Questions How do I report a death to Lincoln Financial? You can report a death online via the Lincoln Financial Claims Portal or by calling (800) 487-1485 (Option 4) for individual life insurance. For employer-sponsored plans, call (800) 423-2765. What is the mailing address for Lincoln Financial death claims? For individual life insurance, mail documents to: Lincoln Financial Group, P.O. Box 26068, Greensboro, NC 27420. For group life insurance, the address is typically P.O. Box 2616, Omaha, NE 68103. Does Lincoln Financial pay interest on death claims? Yes, in many states Lincoln Financial is required to pay interest on the death benefit from the date of death until the date of payment. This will be detailed on your benefit statement. Can I fax my claim documents to Lincoln Financial? Fax acceptance varies by department and claim amount. Ask your claims specialist for a specific fax number. Generally, certified death certificates must be mailed. What is a Lincoln SecuredPlus Account? It is a retained asset account used for paying out larger life insurance claims. Instead of a check, you receive a checkbook to access the funds. You can write a check for the full balance to transfer it to your personal bank.

Option A: Online (Recommended) Visit the Lincoln Financial Claims Portal . You can report a death and start a life insurance or annuity claim directly through the site. Option B: Call the Claims Department Contact the specific department based on the product type: Individual Life Insurance & Annuities: (800) 487-1485 (Select Option 4) Group Life Insurance (Employer Plans): (800) 423-2765 Retirement Plans: (800) 348-4608 Hours: Mon–Fri, 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM ET When you reach an agent, they will verify the policy details and send you a claim packet (Claimant's Statement) via mail or email. Step 3: Submit Required Documents Complete the claim forms provided by the representative. You will typically need to mail these along with the death certificate. For Individual Life & Annuities: Lincoln Financial Group P.O. Box 26068 Greensboro, NC 27420 For Group Life Insurance: The Lincoln National Life Insurance Company P.O. Box 2616 Omaha, NE 68103 (Check the specific address listed on your claim packet, as it may differ based on the subsidiary holding the policy.) Step 4: Wait for Processing After Lincoln Financial receives your documents, expect: Document review: 5–10 business days Benefit processing: 10–15 business days after all requirements are met Payment issuance: Checks are typically mailed within 5 days of approval Step 5: Receive Funds Funds are typically distributed via: Lump sum check Lincoln SecuredPlus Account (a retained asset account for larger amounts) Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) (if selected on the claim form) Required Documents Documents Lincoln Financial Requires The exact documents depend on the policy type and the amount of the benefit. Always Required Document Details Certified death certificate One certified copy (Lincoln may return it upon request, but don't count on it) Claimant's Statement Form provided by Lincoln (e.g., Form CL06541 for life insurance) Valid Photo ID Copy of driver's license or passport for the beneficiary If the Estate is the Beneficiary Document Details Letters Testamentary Court-certified document appointing the Executor Letters of Administration Court-certified document appointing the Administrator (if no will) EIN (Tax ID) For the estate (required for interest reporting) If the Beneficiary is a Trust Document Details Trust Certification Verifying the trust's existence and the trustee's authority Trustee's ID Copy of the trustee's government-issued ID For Annuities Document Details Annuity Claim Form Specific to the contract type (Variable vs. Fixed) Spousal Continuation Form If the surviving spouse wishes to continue the contract Account Types How Lincoln Financial Handles Different Account Types Individual Life Insurance Beneficiaries must file a claim to receive the death benefit. If the policy is within the contestability period (usually the first 2 years), the review process will take longer as Lincoln verifies medical history. Timeline: 10–15 business days (uncontested) Group Life Insurance (Employer-Sponsored) Claims are often initiated through the employer's HR department, but beneficiaries can also contact Lincoln directly. The employer may need to provide proof of active employment/enrollment. Timeline: 10–20 business days Annuities Surviving owners or beneficiaries have several options: Lump sum payout Spousal continuation (spouse takes over the contract) 5-year deferral or Life expectancy payments Timeline: 15–20 business days Retirement Plans (401k, 403b) Distribution depends on the plan rules and the beneficiary's relationship to the deceased. Spouses have more rollover options than non-spouse beneficiaries. Timeline: 15–30 business days Lincoln SecuredPlus Account For life insurance payouts over a certain threshold (often $5,000+), Lincoln may open an interest-bearing draft account in the beneficiary's name rather than sending a single check. You can write checks against this account to access funds. State Considerations How Your State Affects the Lincoln Financial Process Lincoln Financial operates nationally, but state laws impact claims: Interest on Death Benefits: Many states (like IL, NY, PA) require insurers to pay interest on the death benefit from the date of death until payment. Contestability: State laws define the contestability period (usually 2 years) during which Lincoln can investigate the cause of death or application accuracy. Slayer Statutes: All states prevent a beneficiary from collecting if they are responsible for the insured's death. Community Property: In states like CA, TX, and AZ, a spouse may have a claim to a portion of the policy proceeds even if they are not the named beneficiary, if premiums were paid with community funds. Key State Variations State Interest Requirement Contestability Notable Rules New York Yes 2 Years Strict beneficiary protection rules California Yes 2 Years Community property interest applies Florida Yes 2 Years Creditor protection for life insurance proceeds For detailed probate requirements, see our state and county probate guides . Timelines How Long Lincoln Financial Takes to Release Funds Account Type Estimated Timeline Key Factor Life Insurance (Simple) 10–15 business days After all forms & death cert received Life Insurance (Contestable) 2–6 months Medical record review required Group Life 10–20 business days Employer verification often needed Annuities 15–20 business days Complexity of distribution options Retirement Accounts 15–30 business days Plan administrator approval Estate as Beneficiary 4–8 weeks Depends on probate paperwork Note: Timelines start only after Lincoln Financial receives the completed claim packet and certified death certificate. Tips & Pitfalls Common Pitfalls and Tips Check for Multiple Policies The decedent may have had an individual policy and a group policy through work. Ask the agent to search for all policies linked to the decedent's Social Security number. Do Not Close the Account Too Early For annuities and retirement accounts, do not attempt to close the account or move funds before speaking with the estate department. This can trigger unintended taxable events. The "Retained Asset Account" Lincoln often pays large claims via a SecuredPlus Account (a checkbook linked to an account) rather than a single check. Tip: You can write one check for the full balance to move it to your own bank immediately if you prefer. Contestability Period If the policy was purchased less than 2 years ago, Lincoln will investigate the medical history. Tip: Be prepared to sign HIPAA release forms to allow them to access medical records. Interest Payments Check if your state requires interest on the death benefit. Lincoln should include this automatically, but it's worth verifying on the final statement. page_title: How to Handle Nationwide Accounts After a Death: 2026 Guide
mailing_address: Nationwide Life Insurance Company | PO Box 182835 | Columbus, OH 43218-2835
overview: Overview Nationwide is one of the largest insurance and financial services companies in the United States, offering life insurance, annuities, and retirement plans. When a policyholder passes away, the beneficiary or estate representative must notify Nationwide's Annuity and Life CARE Team to initiate a claim. This guide covers the specific process for Nationwide Life Insurance and Annuity death claims, including the dedicated phone numbers, mailing addresses, and required documentation. Nationwide offers an online death notification tool which can speed up the initial reporting process. However, for complex estates or multiple policy types, speaking directly with a representative is often recommended. This guide provides informational guidance only. It is not legal advice, and SwiftProbate is not a law firm. Laws and institutional procedures change — verify current requirements directly with Nationwide and consider consulting a qualified attorney.
notification_process: Notification Process How to Notify Nationwide of a Death Step 1: Gather Initial Information Before contacting Nationwide, have the following ready: Decedent's full legal name and date of birth Date of death Social Security number Policy or contract numbers (if known) Your name and relationship to the decedent A certified copy of the death certificate (for reference) Note: If you do not know the policy number, you can use Nationwide's Lost Policy Search tool. Step 2: Report the Death You can report the death to Nationwide in two ways: Option A: Online Notification (Fastest) Use the Online Death Claim Form to submit the initial report. You will need the policy number and decedent's SSN. Option B: Call the Annuity and Life CARE Team Contact the dedicated claims department: Phone: (800) 848-6331 Hours: Mon–Fri, 8 AM – 8 PM ET When you reach an agent, they will verify the policy details, place a hold on the account to prevent unauthorized activity, and generate a claim kit that will be mailed or emailed to you. Step 3: Submit Required Documents Complete the forms provided in the claim kit. You will typically need to mail these back along with a certified death certificate. Mail Life Insurance Claims to: Nationwide Life Insurance Company PO Box 182835 Columbus, OH 43218-2835 Mail Annuity Claims to: Nationwide Life Insurance Company PO Box 182021 Columbus, OH 43218-2021 Check your specific claim packet for the correct PO Box, as it may vary by product type. Step 4: Wait for Processing After Nationwide receives your documents, the process typically follows this timeline: Initial Contact: A representative will contact you within 3 business days of your initial report to provide guidance. Review: Once forms are received, they are reviewed for completeness. Payment: Upon approval, funds are released via check, EFT, or a retained asset account (J.P. Morgan Concourse).
required_documents: Required Documents Documents Nationwide Requires The specific documents needed depend on the policy type and the beneficiary designation. Always Required Document Details Certified death certificate Original certified copy with a raised seal Claimant's Statement The primary claim form provided in the claim kit Government-issued photo ID Copy of driver's license or passport of the beneficiary If There Is No Named Beneficiary (Estate Claim) Document Details Letters Testamentary Court-certified document appointing the Executor (if there is a will) Letters of Administration Court-certified document appointing the Administrator (if no will) Estate EIN Tax ID number for the estate Trust Claims Document Details Trust Certification Or pages of the Trust Agreement naming the successor trustee Trustee's Statement Specific form for trust beneficiaries Annuity Spousal Continuation Document Details Spousal Continuation Form If the surviving spouse wishes to continue the contract instead of taking a lump sum
account_types: Account Types How Nationwide Handles Different Policy Types Life Insurance Policies Named beneficiaries must file a claim to receive the death benefit. The process is generally straightforward and does not require probate unless the estate is the beneficiary. Timeline: 10–15 business days after receipt of all documents Annuities Annuities may have different payout options, including a lump sum, spousal continuation (for surviving spouses), or annuitization (periodic payments). The tax implications vary significantly based on the option chosen. Timeline: 10–15 business days Retirement Plans (401k / 457) If Nationwide administers the decedent's employer-sponsored retirement plan, the beneficiary rules are governed by the plan document and federal law (ERISA). Spouses often have automatic rights to these assets. Timeline: 15–30 business days Variable Universal Life (VUL) These policies have an investment component. The death benefit is determined as of the date of death, but the cash value may fluctuate until the claim is processed. Timeline: 10–20 business days
state_considerations: State Considerations How Your State Affects the Nationwide Process Nationwide operates in all 50 states, but insurance and probate laws are state-specific. Interest on Death Benefits: Many states require insurance companies to pay interest on the death benefit from the date of death until the date of payment. Contestability Period: If the policy was issued less than two years ago, Nationwide may conduct a more thorough investigation to ensure no material misrepresentations were made on the application. Small Estate Affidavits: If the estate is the beneficiary and the value is low, you may be able to use a state-specific Small Estate Affidavit instead of full probate. Key State Variations State Interest Required? Small Estate Threshold California Yes $184,500 New York Yes $50,000 Florida Yes $75,000 Texas Yes $75,000 (excluding homestead) For detailed probate requirements, see our state and county probate guides .
timelines: Timelines How Long Nationwide Takes to Release Funds Claim Type Estimated Timeline Key Factor Life Insurance (Clean Claim) 10–15 business days All docs submitted, policy >2 years old Life Insurance (Contestable) 30–60+ days Policy <2 years old; medical records review required Annuity Death Benefit 10–15 business days Depends on payout option selection Estate as Beneficiary 3–6 months Depends on court appointment of executor Lost Policy Search 5–10 business days Time to locate records without policy number Note: Nationwide's "CARE Team" aims to contact you within 3 business days of the initial notification to guide you through the timeline.
tips_and_pitfalls: Tips & Pitfalls Common Pitfalls and Tips Check for Multiple Policies Nationwide offers auto, home, life, and retirement products. A notification for a life insurance policy might not automatically trigger a review of a retirement account. Ask the representative to search for all relationships under the decedent's SSN. Do Not Send Original Policy Unless Requested Usually, you do not need to surrender the physical policy document. If you cannot find it, simply indicate it is "lost" on the claim form. Understanding "Retained Asset Accounts" For large settlements, Nationwide may offer a "Retained Asset Account" (often branded as a checking account with J.P. Morgan). This allows you to keep the funds earning interest while you decide what to do. You are not required to keep the money there; you can write a check for the full balance immediately. Contestability Clause If the death occurred within two years of the policy start date, expect a longer process. Nationwide is legally entitled to review the decedent's medical history to ensure the application was accurate. Mail with Tracking Always send original documents like death certificates via Certified Mail with Return Receipt or a trackable service (FedEx/UPS) to the specific PO Box provided in your claim kit.
faq: Frequently Asked Questions How do I report a death to Nationwide? You can report a death online using the Nationwide Death Claim Form or by calling the Annuity and Life CARE Team at (800) 848-6331. What is the mailing address for Nationwide life insurance claims? For life insurance claims, mail documents to: Nationwide Life Insurance Company, PO Box 182835, Columbus, OH 43218-2835. Always verify the address on your specific claim packet. Do I need the original policy to file a claim? No. If you cannot find the original policy, you can simply indicate that it is lost on the Claimant's Statement. This will not delay your claim. How long does it take to get a life insurance check from Nationwide? Once all required documents are received and approved, payment is typically sent within 10 to 15 business days. Claims within the two-year contestability period may take longer. Does Nationwide pay interest on death claims? Yes, in most states, Nationwide pays interest on the death benefit from the date of death until the date of payment, as required by state law. Can I fax my claim documents to Nationwide? Yes, you can fax claim documents to (888) 634-4472. However, if a certified death certificate is required, the original must usually be mailed.

page_title: How to Handle State Farm Accounts After a Death: 2026 Guide
mailing_address: State Farm Life Insurance Company | Life Claims | P.O. Box 2364 | Bloomington, IL 61702-2364
overview: Overview State Farm is the largest property and casualty insurance provider in the United States, widely known for its "good neighbor" agent network. While best known for auto and home insurance, State Farm is a major provider of life insurance and annuities. When a policyholder passes away, the process for claiming benefits depends heavily on the type of product. Life insurance and annuity claims are handled by the Life Claims Operation Office , while mutual funds are managed through State Farm Investment Planning Services . Important Note on Bank Accounts: State Farm Bank deposit accounts (checking, savings, CDs) and credit cards were transitioned to U.S. Bank in 2020–2021. If the deceased held a "State Farm Bank" card or account, you will likely need to contact U.S. Bank directly. This guide focuses on the notification and claim process for State Farm life insurance, annuities, and investment products . This guide provides informational guidance only. It is not legal advice, and SwiftProbate is not a law firm. Laws and institutional procedures change — verify current requirements directly with State Farm and consider consulting a qualified attorney.
notification_process: Notification Process How to Notify State Farm of a Death Step 1: Gather Initial Information Before contacting State Farm, have the following ready: Policy number(s) (look for recent billing statements or the original policy document) Decedent's full legal name and date of birth Date of death Social Security number Your contact information Step 2: Notify State Farm You have three options to start the claim process: Online: Use the Report a Claim tool . This is often the fastest way to start the file. Phone: Call the Life Claims department directly at (877) 292-0398 . Agent: Contact the deceased's local State Farm agent. Agents can be very helpful in navigating the process and submitting documents on your behalf. Step 3: Wait for Review and Claim Forms Once notified, the Life Claims Operation Office will review the policy status. Timeline: Approximately 1–2 business days after notification. Outcome: They will send a Beneficiary Claim Kit via mail or email to all named beneficiaries. Step 4: Submit Required Documents Each beneficiary must complete their specific claim forms. Complete the Claim Form: If you received a "key code" in your letter, you may be able to complete the beneficiary form online . Otherwise, fill out the paper forms. Attach the Death Certificate: A certified copy is usually required. Mail Documents: Send everything to the address provided in your claim kit (typically the Bloomington, IL address). Mail to: State Farm Life Insurance Company Life Claims P.O. Box 2364 Bloomington, IL 61702-2364 Step 5: Receive Funds Once State Farm receives your completed forms and death certificate: Processing Time: Payment is typically processed within 1–3 business days of receipt. Delivery: Check: Arrives in ~10 business days. EFT (Direct Deposit): Arrives in 2–3 business days.
required_documents: Required Documents Documents State Farm Requires The specific documents depend on the policy type and the beneficiary designation. Life Insurance & Annuities Document Details Certified Death Certificate Must be an original certified copy with a raised seal. Claimant's Statement The specific claim form sent to you by State Farm. W-9 Form For tax reporting purposes (often included in the claim form). Trust Certification Required if the beneficiary is a Trust. Letters Testamentary Required if the beneficiary is the "Estate" of the insured. Mutual Funds / Investment Accounts Document Details Certified Death Certificate Required to freeze or transfer the account. Affidavit of Domicile May be required depending on the state of residence. Letter of Instruction Signed by the executor/administrator detailing how to handle the assets. Corporate Resolution / Court Appointment If an executor is acting on behalf of the estate. Auto / Home Insurance Document Details Death Certificate To remove a deceased spouse from a policy or cancel a policy. Executor paperwork To transfer a policy to the estate (e.g., for a car or home that is still being used/maintained).
account_types: Account Types How State Farm Handles Different Account Types Life Insurance Policies State Farm reviews the policy to confirm it was active. If there are named beneficiaries, the proceeds are paid directly to them, bypassing probate. Timeline: Funds usually released 1–3 business days after documents are received. Annuities Similar to life insurance, annuities with named beneficiaries are paid out directly. Options may include a lump sum, continued payments, or a spousal continuation. Timeline: 5–10 business days for processing. Mutual Funds / Investment Accounts These accounts are held through State Farm Investment Planning Services . If a beneficiary is named (TOD - Transfer on Death), assets transfer directly. If no beneficiary is named, the account becomes part of the estate and requires probate documents. Contact: Call (800) 447-4930 for investment accounts. Auto & Home Insurance Policies These policies do not "pay out" upon death but must be managed. Surviving Spouse: Can usually be named the "Named Insured" easily. Estate: The policy can often continue in the name of the estate until the asset (car/home) is sold. Do not cancel insurance on a home or car until it is sold or transferred, as gaps in coverage can be disastrous. State Farm Bank Accounts As noted, these accounts were transferred to U.S. Bank . You must contact U.S. Bank for checking, savings, or credit card issues.
state_considerations: State Considerations How Your State Affects the State Farm Process State Farm operates nationally, but state laws impact the claims process: Contestability Period: If the life insurance policy is less than 2 years old, State Farm may investigate the cause of death (standard industry practice). Interest on Claims: Many states (like IL, NY, CA) require insurers to pay interest on the death benefit from the date of death until the date of payment. Spousal Rights: In community property states (AZ, CA, ID, LA, NV, NM, TX, WA, WI), a spouse may have a claim to a portion of the policy proceeds even if they are not the named beneficiary. Key State Variations State Interest on Claims? Community Property? California Yes Yes Texas Yes Yes Florida Yes No New York Yes No Illinois Yes No For detailed probate requirements, see our state and county probate guides .
timelines: Timelines How Long State Farm Takes to Release Funds State Farm is generally fast with life insurance claims once they have the correct paperwork. Account Type Estimated Timeline Key Factor Life Insurance (Clean Claim) 5–10 business days Total time from notification to check in hand Life Insurance (Contestable) 1–3 months Policies <2 years old require medical record review Annuities 7–14 business days Depends on payout option selected Mutual Funds (TOD) 5–10 business days Transfer to beneficiary Mutual Funds (Estate) 3–6+ weeks Requires probate documents and account re-registration Estate Claim (No Beneficiary) 3–6 months Delayed by probate court process Note: Choosing "EFT/Direct Deposit" instead of a paper check can shave 7–10 days off the timeline.
tips_and_pitfalls: Tips & Pitfalls Common Pitfalls and Tips Check for "Lost" Policies State Farm agents often sold small "burial policies" or term riders on other policies years ago. Ask the agent to search for all policies linked to the decedent's name or SSN, not just the one you have paperwork for. Do Not Cancel Property Insurance Too Soon If the deceased owned a home or car, keep the insurance active . The estate is responsible for protecting these assets. Contact the agent to rewrite the policy in the name of the "Estate of [Name]" or the surviving spouse. The "Key Code" for Online Claims When you receive the claim kit letter, look for a Key Code . Using this code at life.statefarm.com/lbdx/claim allows you to skip mailing some forms and track the status online. Interest is Taxable While the life insurance death benefit itself is generally income-tax-free, any interest paid by State Farm (from date of death to date of payment) is taxable income. You will receive a Form 1099-INT for this interest. U.S. Bank Confusion Do not send banking/credit card death certificates to the State Farm Life Claims address. It will delay processing. Contact U.S. Bank directly for those old State Farm Bank accounts.
faq: Frequently Asked Questions How do I report a death to State Farm? You can report a death online at reportloss.claims.statefarm.com, by calling the Life Claims department at (877) 292-0398, or by contacting the deceased's local State Farm agent. How long does it take to get a life insurance check from State Farm? Once State Farm receives your completed claim forms and death certificate, payment is typically processed in 1–3 business days. If you choose a paper check, allow an additional 7–10 days for mail delivery. Direct deposit takes 2–3 days. Does State Farm require an original death certificate? Yes, State Farm typically requires a certified copy of the death certificate with a raised seal. Photocopies are generally not accepted for life insurance claims. What happens to State Farm Bank accounts? State Farm Bank deposit accounts and credit cards were transferred to U.S. Bank in 2020. You must contact U.S. Bank to handle these accounts. Can I handle the claim through my local agent? Yes. State Farm agents are authorized to help beneficiaries file claims, certify documents in some cases, and forward paperwork to the claims office. This is often the easiest method.

full_page_text: Overview Notification Process Required Documents Account Types State Considerations Timelines Tips & Pitfalls Document Checklist Account Types FAQ Home / Institution Guides / State Farm How to Handle State Farm Accounts After a Death: 2026 Guide This guide is for educational purposes only and is not legal advice. Institutional procedures change — verify current requirements directly with State Farm or consult an attorney. Last updated: February 16, 2026 Your estate is unique — get a personalized task list with deadlines, forms, and next steps Start free Quick Contact Estate Dept. Phone (877) 292-0398 Hours Contact for hours Mailing Address State Farm Life Insurance Company Life Claims P.O. Box 2364 Bloomington, IL 61702-2364 Online Notification Estate Page Overview State Farm is the largest property and casualty insurance provider in the United States, widely known for its "good neighbor" agent network. While best known for auto and home insurance, State Farm is a major provider of life insurance and annuities. When a policyholder passes away, the process for claiming benefits depends heavily on the type of product. Life insurance and annuity claims are handled by the Life Claims Operation Office , while mutual funds are managed through State Farm Investment Planning Services . Important Note on Bank Accounts: State Farm Bank deposit accounts (checking, savings, CDs) and credit cards were transitioned to U.S. Bank in 2020–2021. If the deceased held a "State Farm Bank" card or account, you will likely need to contact U.S. Bank directly. This guide focuses on the notification and claim process for State Farm life insurance, annuities, and investment products . This guide provides informational guidance only. It is not legal advice, and SwiftProbate is not a law firm. Laws and institutional procedures change — verify current requirements directly with State Farm and consider consulting a qualified attorney. Notification Process How to Notify State Farm of a Death Step 1: Gather Initial Information Before contacting State Farm, have the following ready: Policy number(s) (look for recent billing statements or the original policy document) Decedent's full legal name and date of birth Date of death Social Security number Your contact information Step 2: Notify State Farm You have three options to start the claim process: Online: Use the Report a Claim tool . This is often the fastest way to start the file. Phone: Call the Life Claims department directly at (877) 292-0398 . Agent: Contact the deceased's local State Farm agent. Agents can be very helpful in navigating the process and submitting documents on your behalf. Step 3: Wait for Review and Claim Forms Once notified, the Life Claims Operation Office will review the policy status. Timeline: Approximately 1–2 business days after notification. Outcome: They will send a Beneficiary Claim Kit via mail or email to all named beneficiaries. Step 4: Submit Required Documents Each beneficiary must complete their specific claim forms. Complete the Claim Form: If you received a "key code" in your letter, you may be able to complete the beneficiary form online . Otherwise, fill out the paper forms. Attach the Death Certificate: A certified copy is usually required. Mail Documents: Send everything to the address provided in your claim kit (typically the Bloomington, IL address). Mail to: State Farm Life Insurance Company Life Claims P.O. Box 2364 Bloomington, IL 61702-2364 Step 5: Receive Funds Once State Farm receives your completed forms and death certificate: Processing Time: Payment is typically processed within 1–3 business days of receipt. Delivery: Check: Arrives in ~10 business days. EFT (Direct Deposit): Arrives in 2–3 business days. Required Documents Documents State Farm Requires The specific documents depend on the policy type and the beneficiary designation. Life Insurance & Annuities Document Details Certified Death Certificate Must be an original certified copy with a raised seal. Claimant's Statement The specific claim form sent to you by State Farm. W-9 Form For tax reporting purposes (often included in the claim form). Trust Certification Required if the beneficiary is a Trust. Letters Testamentary Required if the beneficiary is the "Estate" of the insured. Mutual Funds / Investment Accounts Document Details Certified Death Certificate Required to freeze or transfer the account. Affidavit of Domicile May be required depending on the state of residence. Letter of Instruction Signed by the executor/administrator detailing how to handle the assets. Corporate Resolution / Court Appointment If an executor is acting on behalf of the estate. Auto / Home Insurance Document Details Death Certificate To remove a deceased spouse from a policy or cancel a policy. Executor paperwork To transfer a policy to the estate (e.g., for a car or home that is still being used/maintained). Account Types How State Farm Handles Different Account Types Life Insurance Policies State Farm reviews the policy to confirm it was active. If there are named beneficiaries, the proceeds are paid directly to them, bypassing probate. Timeline: Funds usually released 1–3 business days after documents are received. Annuities Similar to life insurance, annuities with named beneficiaries are paid out directly. Options may include a lump sum, continued payments, or a spousal continuation. Timeline: 5–10 business days for processing. Mutual Funds / Investment Accounts These accounts are held through State Farm Investment Planning Services . If a beneficiary is named (TOD - Transfer on Death), assets transfer directly. If no beneficiary is named, the account becomes part of the estate and requires probate documents. Contact: Call (800) 447-4930 for investment accounts. Auto & Home Insurance Policies These policies do not "pay out" upon death but must be managed. Surviving Spouse: Can usually be named the "Named Insured" easily. Estate: The policy can often continue in the name of the estate until the asset (car/home) is sold. Do not cancel insurance on a home or car until it is sold or transferred, as gaps in coverage can be disastrous. State Farm Bank Accounts As noted, these accounts were transferred to U.S. Bank . You must contact U.S. Bank for checking, savings, or credit card issues. State Considerations How Your State Affects the State Farm Process State Farm operates nationally, but state laws impact the claims process: Contestability Period: If the life insurance policy is less than 2 years old, State Farm may investigate the cause of death (standard industry practice). Interest on Claims: Many states (like IL, NY, CA) require insurers to pay interest on the death benefit from the date of death until the date of payment. Spousal Rights: In community property states (AZ, CA, ID, LA, NV, NM, TX, WA, WI), a spouse may have a claim to a portion of the policy proceeds even if they are not the named beneficiary. Key State Variations State Interest on Claims? Community Property? California Yes Yes Texas Yes Yes Florida Yes No New York Yes No Illinois Yes No For detailed probate requirements, see our state and county probate guides . Timelines How Long State Farm Takes to Release Funds State Farm is generally fast with life insurance claims once they have the correct paperwork. Account Type Estimated Timeline Key Factor Life Insurance (Clean Claim) 5–10 business days Total time from notification to check in hand Life Insurance (Contestable) 1–3 months Policies <2 years old require medical record review Annuities 7–14 business days Depends on payout option selected Mutual Funds (TOD) 5–10 business days Transfer to beneficiary Mutual Funds (Estate) 3–6+ weeks Requires probate documents and account re-registration Estate Claim (No Beneficiary) 3–6 months Delayed by probate court process Note: Choosing "EFT/Direct Deposit" instead of a paper check can shave 7–10 days off the timeline. Tips & Pitfalls Common Pitfalls and Tips Check for "Lost" Policies State Farm agents often sold small "burial policies" or term riders on other policies years ago. Ask the agent to search for all policies linked to the decedent's name or SSN, not just the one you have paperwork for. Do Not Cancel Property Insurance Too Soon If the deceased owned a home or car, keep the insurance active . The estate is responsible for protecting these assets. Contact the agent to rewrite the policy in the name of the "Estate of [Name]" or the surviving spouse. The "Key Code" for Online Claims When you receive the claim kit letter, look for a Key Code . Using this code at life.statefarm.com/lbdx/claim allows you to skip mailing some forms and track the status online. Interest is Taxable While the life insurance death benefit itself is generally income-tax-free, any interest paid by State Farm (from date of death to date of payment) is taxable income. You will receive a Form 1099-INT for this interest. U.S. Bank Confusion Do not send banking/credit card death certificates to the State Farm Life Claims address. It will delay processing. Contact U.S. Bank directly for those old State Farm Bank accounts. page_title: How to Handle Transamerica Accounts After a Death: 2026 Guide
mailing_address: Transamerica Claims Department | P.O. Box 219 | Cedar Rapids, IA 52406-0219
overnight_address: Transamerica | 6400 C Street SW | Cedar Rapids, IA 52499
overview: Overview Transamerica is one of the largest life insurance and retirement companies in the United States, serving millions of customers with insurance policies, annuities, and investment accounts. When a policyholder passes away, the beneficiary or estate representative must notify Transamerica to initiate the claim process. This guide covers Transamerica's estate services and claims department, the step-by-step notification process, required documents for life insurance and annuity claims, and realistic timelines. Because Transamerica has acquired many other insurers over the years (such as Monumental Life and Stonebridge Life), this process generally applies to those legacy policies as well. Transamerica offers a dedicated online "First Report of Death" tool which is often the fastest way to start. This guide provides informational guidance only. It is not legal advice, and SwiftProbate is not a law firm. Laws and institutional procedures change — verify current requirements directly with Transamerica and consider consulting a qualified attorney.
notification_process: Notification Process How to Notify Transamerica of a Death Step 1: Gather Initial Information Before contacting Transamerica, have the following ready: Deceased's full legal name and date of birth Date of death Social Security number Policy or account numbers (check recent statements or premium bills) Beneficiary information (names, addresses, SSNs) Certified copy of the death certificate (you will need to mail this later) Step 2: Initiate the Claim You can start the process online or by phone. Option A: Online (Recommended) Use the First Report of Death Claim tool. You will enter the policyholder's details and your contact information. Transamerica will then mail or email the specific claim packets required. Option B: Call Estate Services Contact Transamerica's life insurance and claims line: Phone: (800) 797-2643 Hours: Mon–Fri, 8 AM – 7 PM ET Note: If calling about a retirement plan (401k) specifically, call (800) 755-5801. When you reach an agent, they will: Verify the policyholder's identity Confirm the beneficiaries on file Open a claim case Send the appropriate Claimant's Statement forms to each beneficiary Step 3: Submit Required Documents Once you receive the claim packet, complete the Claimant's Statement and gather the required documents. Mail to: Transamerica Claims Department P.O. Box 219 Cedar Rapids, IA 52406-0219 For Overnight/Express Mail: Transamerica 6400 C Street SW Cedar Rapids, IA 52499 Step 4: Wait for Processing After Transamerica receives your documents, expect: Acknowledgment: 3–5 business days via email or mail Review & Processing: 10–15 business days Payment: Sent via check or direct deposit once approved Step 5: Receive Funds Life insurance proceeds are typically tax-free. Annuity death benefits may have tax implications. Transamerica may offer a "Retained Asset Account" (a checking account for the proceeds) for large amounts, or a lump sum check.
required_documents: Required Documents Documents Transamerica Requires The exact documents depend on the policy type and the amount of the benefit. Always Required Document Details Certified death certificate Original with raised seal (Transamerica may not return this) Claimant's Statement Form provided by Transamerica; each beneficiary must sign one Government-issued photo ID Copy of driver's license or passport for the claimant Often Required (Policy Dependent) Document Details Original Policy Contract If available (if lost, check the "Policy Lost" box on the claim form) HIPAA Authorization Required for contestable claims (policies <2 years old) or specific health riders Trust Certification If the beneficiary is a Trust Estate Documents Letters Testamentary if the beneficiary is "The Estate" If There Is No Named Beneficiary If the named beneficiary is deceased or no beneficiary was named, the proceeds typically go to the Estate . Document Details Letters Testamentary / Administration Issued by probate court appointing the Executor EIN (Tax ID) For the Estate of the deceased Annuity Accounts Document Details Annuity Claim Form Specific to investment products; outlines tax withholding options W-9 Form For tax reporting purposes
account_types: Account Types How Transamerica Handles Different Account Types Life Insurance Policies Life insurance is the most common Transamerica product. Process: Beneficiaries file a claim; proceeds are paid directly to them. Probate: Not required if a living beneficiary is named. Timeline: 10–15 business days after receipt of documents. Annuities Annuities stop regular payments upon death. The "death benefit" (remainder of the contract) is paid to the beneficiary. Taxation: Unlike life insurance, gains in an annuity are taxable to the beneficiary. Options: Lump sum, spousal continuation (if spouse is beneficiary), or 5-year deferral. Timeline: 15–20 business days. Retirement Plans (401k / IRA) If Transamerica manages an employer 401(k) or individual IRA: Process: Beneficiary must choose a distribution option (roll over to inherited IRA, lump sum, etc.). Documents: Death certificate + Distribution Election Form. Timeline: 10–20 business days. Legacy Policies (Monumental, Stonebridge, etc.) Transamerica administers many older policies from acquired companies. Process: Same as Transamerica policies. Use the Transamerica claim forms and contact numbers. Note: Look for the original company name on the policy to ensure you reference it correctly when calling.
state_considerations: State Considerations How Your State Affects the Transamerica Process Transamerica operates nationally, but state laws impact claims: Interest on Claims: Many states (like IL, NY, CA) require insurers to pay interest on the death benefit from the date of death until payment. Contestability: If the policy is less than 2 years old, Transamerica has the right to investigate the medical history (contestability period). This is standard state insurance law. Small Estate Affidavits: If the proceeds are payable to the estate and the amount is small, you may be able to use a state-specific Small Estate Affidavit instead of full probate. Key State Variations State Interest Payable? Small Estate Threshold California Yes $208,850 New York Yes $50,000 Florida Yes $75,000 Texas Yes $75,000 (excluding homestead) For detailed probate requirements, see our state and county probate guides .
timelines: Timelines How Long Transamerica Takes to Release Funds Account Type Estimated Timeline Key Factor Life Insurance (Clean Claim) 10–15 business days All docs received, policy >2 years old Life Insurance (Contestable) 3–6 months Policy <2 years old; medical records review required Annuity Death Benefit 15–20 business days Tax election forms often cause delays Retirement (401k/IRA) 10–20 business days Depends on plan rules and distribution choice Estate/Probate Claim 3–6+ months Depends on court issuing Letters Testamentary Note: Missing signatures or illegible death certificates are the most common reasons for delay.
tips_and_pitfalls: Tips & Pitfalls Common Pitfalls and Tips Do Not Ignore the "Contestability" Clause If the policy was purchased within 2 years of the death, Transamerica will likely conduct a medical investigation. This is normal. Do not be alarmed, but expect a longer timeline (months vs. weeks). Check for Multiple Policies Transamerica has acquired many companies (Monumental Life, Peoples Benefit, Western Reserve). The deceased may have multiple small policies. Ask the agent to search by Social Security Number to find all coverage. Interest is Taxable While the life insurance principal is tax-free, any interest paid by Transamerica (from date of death to payment) is taxable income. You will receive a Form 1099-INT for this interest. Keep the "Policy Number" Handy Always write the policy number on every document you mail to Transamerica. This prevents your death certificate from getting lost in their mailroom. Send Certified Mail Always send original documents like death certificates via Certified Mail with Return Receipt . This provides proof of delivery.
faq: Frequently Asked Questions How do I report a death to Transamerica? You can report a death online using the 'First Report of Death' tool at myta.transamerica.com, or by calling their estate services line at (800) 797-2643. What is the mailing address for Transamerica death claims? Mail claim forms and death certificates to: Transamerica Claims Department, P.O. Box 219, Cedar Rapids, IA 52406-0219. Does Transamerica require an original death certificate? Yes, Transamerica typically requires one certified copy of the death certificate with a raised seal. They may not return this document, so order extra copies. How long does it take to get a life insurance payout from Transamerica? Once all documents are received and in good order, standard claims are processed in 10–15 business days. Contestable claims (policies less than 2 years old) take significantly longer. What if I can't find the original policy? You do not strictly need the original policy to file a claim. On the Claimant's Statement, simply check the box indicating the policy is lost. Transamerica will proceed with their internal records.

Check for Multiple Policies Transamerica has acquired many companies (Monumental Life, Peoples Benefit, Western Reserve). The deceased may have multiple small policies. Ask the agent to search by Social Security Number to find all coverage. Interest is Taxable While the life insurance principal is tax-free, any interest paid by Transamerica (from date of death to payment) is taxable income. You will receive a Form 1099-INT for this interest. Keep the "Policy Number" Handy Always write the policy number on every document you mail to Transamerica. This prevents your death certificate from getting lost in their mailroom. Send Certified Mail Always send original documents like death certificates via Certified Mail with Return Receipt . This provides proof of delivery. Your estate is unique — get a personalized task list with deadlines, forms, and next steps Start free Document Checklist Document When Required How to Obtain Certified death certificate Always County vital records office or funeral home Claimant's Statement Always Provided by Transamerica (mail or download) Government-issued photo ID Always Driver's license or passport of beneficiary Original Policy Contract If available Deceased's personal records (check 'Lost Policy' if unavailable) Trust Certification If beneficiary is a Trust Prepared by Trustee or Attorney Letters Testamentary If beneficiary is the Estate Probate Court HIPAA Authorization Contestable claims (policy <2 years old) Provided by Transamerica in claim packet Account Types at a Glance Account Type Process Est. Timeline Probate? Life Insurance Beneficiary submits claim form & death cert; funds paid tax-free 10–15 business days No Annuity Beneficiary claims death benefit; tax options selected 15–20 business days No Retirement (IRA/401k) Beneficiary chooses distribution method (lump sum vs rollover) 10–20 business days No Estate Claim

mailing_address: Equitable | PO Box 1047 | Charlotte, NC 28201-1047

overview: Overview Equitable (formerly AXA Equitable) is a major financial services company providing life insurance, annuities, and investment products to millions of Americans. When a policyholder or account holder passes away, the beneficiary or estate representative must notify Equitable to initiate the claim process for life insurance benefits or transfer investment assets. This guide covers Equitable's death claim process, including specific contact numbers for life insurance and annuities, required forms, and what to expect regarding timelines. Equitable operates nationally, so state-specific probate requirements may apply depending on where the decedent lived and the type of assets held. Equitable offers an online "Start a Claim" portal to streamline the notification process. This guide provides informational guidance only. It is not legal advice, and SwiftProbate is not a law firm. Laws and institutional procedures change — verify current requirements directly with Equitable and consider consulting a qualified attorney.
notification_process: Notification Process How to Notify Equitable of a Death Step 1: Gather Initial Information Before contacting Equitable, have the following ready: Decedent's full legal name and date of birth Date of death Social Security number Policy or Account numbers (if known) Your name and relationship to the decedent A certified copy of the death certificate Step 2: Contact the Appropriate Department Equitable has specific phone numbers depending on the product type. If you are unsure, start with the Life Insurance line. Life Insurance Claims: (800) 777-6510 Annuity Claims: (800) 789-7771 EQUI-VEST Claims: (800) 628-6673 Equitable Advisors (Brokerage): (866) 487-7484 Hours: Mon–Thu 8:30 AM – 7:00 PM, Fri 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM ET You can also initiate a claim online via the Equitable Start a Claim page . When you reach an agent, they will: Verify the decedent's identity and policy status Place a deceased flag on the account(s) Provide a claim number or reference number Explain which documents are needed (typically a "Claim for Death Benefits" form) Send you a claim packet via mail or email Step 3: Submit Required Documents Mail the completed forms and death certificate to Equitable's processing center. Regular Mail: Equitable PO Box 1047 Charlotte, NC 28201-1047 Overnight / Express Mail: Equitable National Operations Center 8501 IBM Drive, Suite 150 Charlotte, NC 28262-4333 Step 4: Wait for Processing After Equitable receives your documents, expect: Online/Mobile submissions: Processed in approximately 5–10 business days Mail submissions: Processed in approximately 7–14 business days (plus mailing time) Step 5: Receive Funds Once the claim is approved, funds are distributed according to the beneficiary's instructions (e.g., check, electronic funds transfer, or a retained asset account).
required_documents: Required Documents Documents Equitable Requires The exact documents depend on the policy type and beneficiary designation. Always Required Document Details Certified death certificate Original certified copy required Claim for Death Benefits Also known as the Claimant's Statement (provided by Equitable) Original Policy If available (not always mandatory if lost) If There Is a Will (Testate Estate) Document Details Letters Testamentary Required if the estate is the beneficiary EIN (Employer Identification Number) For the estate, if opening an estate account If There Is No Will (Intestate Estate) Document Details Letters of Administration Issued by the probate court if the estate is the beneficiary Trust Accounts / Trust Beneficiaries Document Details Trust Certification Verifying the trust's existence and trustee's authority Trustee ID Government-issued photo ID of the trustee Small Estate (Below Probate Threshold) Document Details Small estate affidavit May be accepted for smaller annuity/brokerage balances if no beneficiary is named (state-specific)
account_types: Account Types How Equitable Handles Different Account Types Life Insurance Policies Beneficiaries must file a Claim for Death Benefits . If the beneficiary is a named individual, probate is generally not required. If the beneficiary is "The Estate," the executor must provide court appointments. Timeline: 5–10 business days (online) or 7–14 business days (mail) Annuities Named beneficiaries can choose to receive a lump sum, continue the annuity (spousal continuation), or take distributions over time. A death certificate and claim form are required. Timeline: 7–14 business days Equitable Advisors (Brokerage Accounts) For investment accounts held through Equitable Advisors, the process involves transferring assets to the beneficiary or estate. If a Transfer on Death (TOD) beneficiary is named, assets transfer directly. Otherwise, probate documents (Letters Testamentary) are usually required. Timeline: 2–4 weeks EQUI-VEST Accounts These are variable annuity products often used in retirement plans. Beneficiaries must contact the specific EQUI-VEST line ((800) 628-6673) to discuss distribution options and tax implications. Timeline: 7–14 business days
state_considerations: State Considerations How Your State Affects the Equitable Process Equitable operates nationally, but probate and insurance laws are governed by state law . Your state determines: Interest on death benefits — some states require insurers to pay interest from the date of death Small estate thresholds — estates below certain values can skip formal probate Community property rules — in states like AZ, CA, ID, LA, NV, NM, TX, WA, and WI, spouses may have rights to policies even if not named Timeliness of claims — states often mandate how quickly insurers must pay clean claims Key State Variations State Small Estate Threshold Community Property? Notable Rules California $184,500 Yes Interest payable from date of death New York $50,000 No Strict timelines for claim payment Texas $75,000 Yes Affidavit of Heirship often used For detailed probate requirements, see our state and county probate guides .
timelines: Timelines How Long Equitable Takes to Release Funds Account Type Estimated Timeline Key Factor Life Insurance (Clean Claim) 5–10 business days Online submission is faster Life Insurance (Mail) 7–14 business days Plus mailing time Annuity Death Claim 7–14 business days Depends on distribution option selected Brokerage (TOD) 2–3 weeks Requires new account setup Estate Claim (Probate) 3–6+ months Depends on court issuing Letters Testamentary Note: Claims within the 2-year contestability period (if the policy was less than 2 years old) may take significantly longer to investigate.
tips_and_pitfalls: Tips & Pitfalls Common Pitfalls and Tips Check for Multiple Policies Equitable has acquired other companies over the years (e.g., MONY Life Insurance). Ask the agent to search for all policies linked to the decedent's Social Security number. Do Not Spend Insurance Proceeds Immediately If you are the beneficiary, the money is yours, but ensure you understand any tax implications (especially for annuities, which may have taxable gains) before spending. Life insurance death benefits are generally income-tax-free. Use the Online Portal Submitting documents via Equitable's online portal is significantly faster than regular mail and provides a digital record of your submission. Keep the Claim Number Handy You will be assigned a claim or case number during your first call. Write this down and include it on every document you mail or upload. Contestability Period If the policy was issued less than two years ago, Equitable may investigate the medical history of the decedent. This is standard procedure but can delay payment by several months.
faq: Frequently Asked Questions How do I report a death to Equitable? You can report a death by calling their Life Insurance Claims line at (800) 777-6510 or by visiting the 'Start a Claim' page on the Equitable website. What is the mailing address for Equitable death claims? For regular mail: Equitable, PO Box 1047, Charlotte, NC 28201-1047. For overnight delivery: Equitable National Operations Center, 8501 IBM Drive, Suite 150, Charlotte, NC 28262-4333. How long does it take to get a life insurance payout from Equitable? Once all documents are received and in good order, online submissions are typically processed in 5–10 business days, while mailed claims take 7–14 business days. Does Equitable require an original death certificate? Yes, Equitable typically requires a certified copy of the death certificate. They may return it upon request, but it is best to ask the agent specifically if you need it back. What if the beneficiary is 'The Estate'? If the estate is the beneficiary, the executor or administrator must provide court-certified Letters Testamentary or Letters of Administration and an EIN for the estate.

page_title: How to Handle Pacific Life Accounts After a Death: 2026 Guide
mailing_address: Pacific Life | P.O. Box 2030 | Omaha, NE 68103-2030
overnight_address: Pacific Life | 6750 Mercy Rd, 5th Floor | Omaha, NE 68106
overview: Overview Pacific Life is one of the leading life insurance and annuity companies in the United States, serving millions of policyholders. When a policyholder or annuitant passes away, the beneficiary or estate representative must notify Pacific Life to initiate the claim process. This guide covers Pacific Life's specific procedures for life insurance policies and annuity contracts. Note that Pacific Life has distinct contact points depending on whether the product is a standard life insurance policy, a "Lynchburg" policy (prefix "2L"), or an annuity. Pacific Life offers an online claim initiation tool which is often the fastest way to start the process. This guide provides informational guidance only. It is not legal advice, and SwiftProbate is not a law firm. Laws and institutional procedures change — verify current requirements directly with Pacific Life and consider consulting a qualified attorney.
notification_process: Notification Process How to Notify Pacific Life of a Death Step 1: Identify the Policy Type Pacific Life routes claims based on the policy number. Check the policy documents to determine which department to contact: General Life Insurance: Standard policies. Lynchburg Operations: Policy numbers starting with "2L" . Annuities: Fixed or variable annuity contracts. Group/Workforce Benefits: Policies provided through an employer. Step 2: Gather Initial Information Before contacting Pacific Life, have the following ready: Decedent's full legal name and date of birth Date of death Social Security number Policy or Contract numbers Beneficiary information (names, addresses, SSNs) Certified copy of the death certificate (will be required later) Step 3: Initiate the Claim You can start the process online or by phone. Option A: Online (Fastest for Life Insurance) Visit the Pacific Life Online Claim Form to submit the initial notification. Option B: Call the Specific Department * General Life Insurance: (800) 347-7787 Hours:* Mon–Fri, 6 AM – 5 PM PT * Lynchburg Operations (Policies starting with "2L"): (844) 276-0193 Hours:* Mon–Fri, 6 AM – 5 PM PT * Annuities: (800) 722-4448 Hours:* Mon–Fri, 6 AM – 5 PM PT * Workforce Benefits (Group Life): (855) 810-3301 Step 4: Submit Required Documents After the initial notification, Pacific Life will send a Beneficiary Claim Statement (claim packet). You must complete this and return it with a certified death certificate. Mailing Address for General Life Insurance: Pacific Life P.O. Box 2030 Omaha, NE 68103-2030 Overnight Address for General Life Insurance: Pacific Life 6750 Mercy Rd, 5th Floor Omaha, NE 68106 Mailing Address for Lynchburg Operations: Pacific Life Insurance Company – Lynchburg Operations PO Box 2873 Omaha, NE 68103-2873 Overnight Address for Lynchburg Operations: Pacific Life Insurance Company – Lynchburg Operations 6750 Mercy Rd, Ste B Omaha, NE 68106 Mailing Address for Annuities: Pacific Life P.O. Box 2378 Omaha, NE 68103-2378 Overnight Address for Annuities: Pacific Life 6750 Mercy Rd, 4th Floor, RSD Omaha, NE 68106 Step 5: Wait for Processing Once documents are received, Pacific Life typically reviews standard claims within 10–15 business days . If the policy is less than two years old (contestable period), the review may take longer.
required_documents: Required Documents Documents Pacific Life Requires The specific documents depend on the product type and the beneficiary designation. Always Required Document Details Certified death certificate Original certified copy with a raised seal Beneficiary Claim Statement Form provided by Pacific Life after notification Government-issued photo ID Copy of ID for the beneficiary/claimant If the Beneficiary is the Estate Document Details Letters Testamentary / Administration Court-issued document appointing the executor/administrator EIN (Employer Identification Number) Tax ID for the estate Estate Claim Form Specific version of the claim form for estates If the Beneficiary is a Trust Document Details Trust Certification Verifies the trust's existence and trustee's authority Trustee ID Copy of the trustee's government ID Annuity Specifics Document Details Original Contract Sometimes requested, but often not mandatory if lost Spousal Continuation Form If a surviving spouse wishes to continue the annuity
account_types: Account Types How Pacific Life Handles Different Policies Life Insurance (Term, Whole, Universal) Named beneficiaries file a claim directly. The death benefit is generally paid out tax-free. * Process: Submit Claim Statement + Death Certificate. * Timeline: 10–15 business days for clean claims. Annuities (Fixed, Variable, Indexed) Annuities have different settlement options. A surviving spouse may have the option to continue the contract ("Spousal Continuation") rather than taking a lump sum. Non-spouse beneficiaries typically must take a distribution, which may have tax consequences. * Process: Submit Claim Statement + Death Certificate + Settlement Election. * Timeline: 10–20 business days. Lynchburg Operations Policies (Prefix "2L") These are specific blocks of business administered separately. It is crucial to use the specific PO Box (2873) and phone number ((844) 276-0193) to avoid delays. * Process: Same as general life, but routed differently. Group / Workforce Benefits If the policy was provided through an employer, the employer's HR department often assists with the initial notification, but the beneficiary must still file the claim with Pacific Life's Workforce Benefits division. * Phone: (855) 810-3301
state_considerations: State Considerations How Your State Affects the Pacific Life Process Pacific Life operates nationally, but state laws impact the claims process: Interest on Death Benefits: Many states require insurers to pay interest on the death benefit from the date of death until the date of payment. Contestability Period: If the death occurs within the first two years of the policy, Pacific Life has the right to investigate the medical history to ensure the application was accurate. This is standard across all states but governed by state insurance codes. Community Property: In states like California, Texas, Arizona, Idaho, Louisiana, Nevada, New Mexico, Washington, and Wisconsin, a spouse may have a claim to a portion of the policy proceeds even if they are not the named beneficiary, depending on how premiums were paid. Key State Variations State Interest Requirement Contestability New York Interest required from date of death 2 years California Interest required from date of death 2 years Florida Interest required from date of death 2 years For detailed probate and estate requirements, see our state and county probate guides .
timelines: Timelines How Long Pacific Life Takes to Release Funds Policy Type Estimated Timeline Key Factor Life Insurance (Standard) 10–15 business days After all documents are received in good order Life Insurance (Contestable) 30–60+ days Death within 2 years of policy issue; requires medical record review Annuities 10–20 business days Depends on settlement option selected Group Life 10–15 business days Employer verification may add time Estate as Beneficiary 3–6+ months Depends on probate court issuing Letters Testamentary Note: Missing signatures or unclear beneficiary designations are the most common reasons for delays.
tips_and_pitfalls: Tips & Pitfalls Common Pitfalls and Tips Check the Policy Number Prefix Sending "Lynchburg" (2L...) policy documents to the general processing center in Omaha (PO Box 2030) will cause significant delays. Always verify the correct PO Box for your specific policy type. Do Not Ignore the "Settlement Options" For annuities and large life insurance policies, you may have options other than a lump sum check, such as a Retained Asset Account or installment payments. Review these carefully to understand the tax implications. Contestability Period If the policy was purchased less than two years ago, expect an investigation. Pacific Life will request medical records to verify the application was truthful. This is not a denial, but a standard industry process. Spousal Continuation (Annuities) If you are a surviving spouse inheriting an annuity, ask about "Spousal Continuation." This allows you to take over the contract tax-deferred, whereas cashing it out might trigger a large tax bill. Keep Copies of Everything Send all physical documents via Certified Mail with Return Receipt . Pacific Life processes thousands of documents daily; having proof of delivery is essential.
faq: Frequently Asked Questions How do I report a death to Pacific Life? You can report a death online at life.myaccount.pacificlife.com/policyowner/ClaimForm or by calling (800) 347-7787 for general life insurance. For annuities, call (800) 722-4448. What is the mailing address for Pacific Life death claims? For standard life insurance: Pacific Life, P.O. Box 2030, Omaha, NE 68103-2030. For 'Lynchburg' policies (prefix 2L): P.O. Box 2873, Omaha, NE 68103-2873. How long does it take to get a life insurance check from Pacific Life? Once all required documents (claim form and death certificate) are received in good order, payment is typically issued within 10 to 15 business days. Does Pacific Life pay interest on death claims? Yes, in most states, Pacific Life pays interest on the death benefit from the date of death until the date of payment, as required by state law. What if the policy is less than two years old? If the insured died within two years of the policy issue date, the claim enters a 'contestability period.' Pacific Life will review medical records to ensure the application was accurate. This process can take 30–60 days or longer.
